reasoning: The Fed is currently in an easing (cutting) cycle. With the SOFR at 4.310% in late October 2025, they are likely still planning gradual rate cuts (e.g., in December or January). If a cut occurs, the SOFR will be substantially lower in February 2026 than in November 2025 (chance of increase ≈ 0%). If the Fed holds rates steady, daily fluctuations in the SOFR make it roughly a 45-50% chance to be strictly higher. Given a decent probability of further cuts and a very low probability of unexpected hikes over this 3-month window, the chance of a SOFR increase is relatively low. I estimate the probability of at least one hold leading to a slight noise-driven increase or an unexpected hike at around 25%.
